sources.title: Map of the town of Concord, Middlesex County Mass
sources.creator: Henry Francis Walling
sources.why_it_matters: Closest high-value 19th-century Concord map found so far; the Library of Congress metadata says it includes an ancillary Concord village map and White Pond/Walden Pond surveys by H. D. Thoreau.

sources.title: Sanborn Fire Insurance Map from Concord, Middlesex County, Massachusetts
sources.creator: Sanborn Map Company
sources.why_it_matters: Later than Fuller's 1860 Concord scenes, but useful for building footprints, street names, and continuity checks in the village center.

sources.title: Sanborn Fire Insurance Map from Concord, Middlesex County, Massachusetts
sources.creator: Sanborn Map Company
sources.why_it_matters: Later continuity check for Concord village structures and addresses.

sources.title: Roxbury to Concord. Roads & distances, &c
sources.why_it_matters: Older road context for Boston-Concord and Lexington-Concord geography; not a direct 1860 source.

sources.title: A Walk Along Concord's American Mile
sources.why_it_matters: Useful current visit-oriented source for surviving Concord sites; it identifies Concord's Italianate Town House at 22 Monument Square as built in 1851 and notes the First Parish reproduction/bell context.

sources.title: Historic Concord: Plan Your Visit - Summer 2026
sources.why_it_matters: Gives current addresses/contact points for visitable Concord sites such as the library, museum, North Bridge, Old Hill, Old Manse, Emerson House, Sleepy Hollow, Thoreau Farm, Walden Pond, Wayside, and Wright Tavern.

sources.title: Saving Concord's Darwinian & Abolitionist landmark, where Thoreau met John Brown
sources.why_it_matters: Identifies the Channing-Fuller-Sanborn / Samuel Jones House at 325 Main Street as a surviving private residence and abolitionist/Darwinian landmark; requires historical-map/deed reconciliation with Fuller's Sudbury Road wording.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap Nominatim
sources.why_it_matters: Provides candidate OSM objects and reverse checks, but does not by itself confirm historical identity.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap
sources.why_it_matters: Target map/database for modern OSM object links; historical locations still require external confirmation before precise coordinates are accepted.
